name: SecureVector — AI Agent with sub-workflow tool gating

nodes.name: SV — Scan input prompt
nodes.notes: Block-on-threat ON. If the user prompt is a known injection / jailbreak, the workflow halts before the agent sees it.

options.systemMessage: You are a helpful assistant with access to a tool that can read files from the user's machine. When the user asks about a file, configuration, log, or document, call `secure_read_file` with the path. When the tool returns {blocked: true, reason}, apologize politely, explain the read was denied by policy, and stop — do not retry the same path.

nodes.name: AI Agent
nodes.notes: promptType=define + text expression — required because the SV Scan Prompt node sits between Chat Trigger and this Agent and does not pass through the chatInput field. We reference the original Chat Trigger output by node name instead of relying on auto-detection.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model (attach your credential)
nodes.notes: Attach your OpenAI / Anthropic / Ollama credential here. Any chat model that supports tool-calling works.

parameters.name: secure_read_file
parameters.description: Read a file from the local filesystem. Use this whenever the user asks about a file, config, log, README, or document on disk. Pass the absolute or relative path on the `path` argument. Returns {blocked: true, reason} when SecureVector policy denies the read — apologize to the user and stop.

nodes.name: Call n8n Workflow Tool — secure_read_file
nodes.notes: Set workflowId to the imported `test-workflow-real-tool-stub.json` workflow. The LLM only ever picks `secure_read_file` — it cannot reach the underlying file-read node directly. The sub-workflow runs SV Check Permission (tool_id=File.read) before invoking the real tool, returning {blocked, reason} on deny.

nodes.name: SV — Cost track
nodes.notes: Demo-only. Token values are hardcoded (200 input / 80 output) to keep this example runnable without an n8n API key. Production tracking against an AI Agent needs source=agent_execution + a key (Settings → API → Create API Key) so the node can read real tokenUsage via /api/v1/executions. For non-Agent flows (OpenAI Message a Model, Basic LLM Chain) source=openai_native or langchain_chain reads tokens from $json directly with no extra credential.
